reviews.review_msg: Great proff. Quirky, but really cares and is very knowledgable. I would def take her upper divclasses if i can

Enjoyed my class with Professor Armijo. She definitely knows her stuff and lectures are well put together. I learned a lot from this class. She cares a lot about her students and it shows. I found the marking pretty easy and the workload managable (participation + midterm + 3 essays). I'd take a class with her again

Interesting and thorough lectures, she genuinely wants us to learn and engage with the material so maybe some students who don't feel passionate about the topics wouldn't do well. Whenever I have time, I go to her office hours and talk to her for an hour and a half, she's very welcoming, insightful, and open to discuss and listen.

She's a great prof and teacher. I took her Money and Power IS course a couple years ago. still refer to the material in order to understand current events. She is a passionate woman with a lot of valuable life experience. Not a cookie cutter prof, I think that where a lot of the negative reviews come from, you'll leave the class enriched!

She will never be able to tell you why you got the grade you got, so you can't fight for it. Her lectures are pretty boring and you can def miss them, but not the tutorials. Lots of writing assignments. Textbooks aren't awful, but some were pretty bad and boring.
TERRIBLE. Kind woman. Got a good grade but unclear in expecatations. Really just a terrible course with no sufficient guide lines. She is a nice woman though. Nothing personal just very unclear

Professor Armijo is clearly extremely passionate about international relations, and it makes classes very engaging and fun. She takes time to make sure everyone is on board before moving on from a topic, and sometimes this means we linger for a long time on things I think most of us get. Nonetheless, a very accomplished, caring, and engaging prof!
Goes on rants that are completely irrelevant, but she cares. Talk to her about literally anything, she is sweet. But not a good prof.
Clear exams that are easy to do half decent on, very approachable and passionate.
As mentioned,kind and knowledgeable professor - but unclear expectations and very difficult to achieve high marks because of this

Leslie's knowledgeable. She's super available, so go to her office hours! She has unclear and confusing expectations. Midterm average was a B-, paper average C+. Treated us as a 4th-year students. Lectures can get boring. She grades exams as a whole but not like one point per answer. HARD MARKER. If you're aiming for an A, it will be really hard!

Professor Armijo is very knowledgeable about international finance and global governance. She is also very supportive of her students. In this particular course, the assignments were often confusing and the expectations unclear. Perhaps more of a focus on policy analysis in the seminars would have been beneficial.

It takes a bit of investigating to figure out what she wants from her assignments, but I always gain a much deeper understanding of topics from them. They're well designed. she's subtlety an excellent prof. boring lectures and annoyingly liberal readings  but allows thoughtful critique, which inspires understanding those ideas & alternatives.

The pinnacle of a 'meh' prof. Lectures aren't all that interesting, neither are the readings she gives out. She'll often bring up background 'facts' and get dates wrong. If you've taken any history classes you'll be annoyed when she perpetuates misnomers of historical events
